name: Rahat Sayyed
summary: Software Engineer with 3+ years of experience in frontend architecture, contract-driven API design, AI-integrated systems and blockchain; architected a system-wide state management refactor resulting in improved loading by 11 seconds, and optimized deployment workflows decreasing development build time by ~10 minutes per cycle.

experience.title: Software Engineer
description: Led the architecture and implementation of a modular, policy-driven governance layer centralizing MCP access control and model I/O auditing, enabling consistent enforcement across agent workflows and reducing policy misconfiguration issues by ~50%.
Designed multi-policy schemas and contract-driven TypeScript APIs, reducing integration-related regressions by 70% as AI interfaces evolved.
Directed a system-wide refactor of React state management, eliminating 9+ redundant API calls and reducing initial load time by 11 seconds.

Built a unified violations ingestion pipeline across LLM, MCP and RAG flows, standardizing cross-source observability reducing incident investigation time by approximately 40% through centralized event tracing.
Developed D3-based interactive risk analytics dashboards to distinguish LLM vs. tool exposure patterns, accelerating policy debugging and strengthening audit traceability across 12+ policies.

Optimized Docker-based frontend build pipeline on AWS EC2 by restructuring dependency installation strategy and leveraging layer caching, reducing development build times by ~10 minutes and accelerating backend frontend integration cycles.

Developed role-based authentication and onboarding in Next.js with tenant-level access boundaries, eliminating post-login misrouting and reducing user-reported login errors by 30% within the first month post release.

Coordinated cross-functional efforts between frontend, backend, and AI teams, integrating 3+ evolving model capabilities with governance constraints, maintaining consistent policy enforcement as the platform introduced new tools and agents.

experience.title: Web 3.0 Developer
description: Designed and deployed Aiken-based smart contracts for a carbon credit marketplace, refactoring validation logic to reduce transaction failures by 80%.
Optimized smart contract architecture for Karbon Ledger, reducing transaction fees by ~35% through script refactoring and execution efficiency improvements.
Built secure Next.js frontend integrations using Lucid.js and Mesh.js, connecting 8+ Cardano smart contracts to a Haskell based backend and REST services.
Performed smart contract reviews and testing to identify edge cases and failure scenarios, improving reliability before mainnet deployment and identifying 8 vulnerabilities.
